platform_decisions.content: Hook system supports four event types: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, UserPromptSubmit with distinct semantics
Hook commands registered with timeout field (integer seconds); hookify uses 10s default
Hook input received exclusively via JSON on stdin with common fields plus event-specific fields
Hook output is JSON to stdout; shape determines effect (empty={} allow, systemMessage=warn, permissionDecision=deny for block)
Blocking PreToolUse requires nested structure: hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision='deny' with matching hookEventName
Stop event uses different blocking schema (decision:'block') than tool events (permissionDecision:'deny')
PostToolUse hooks cannot block operations; exit 2 is ignored; observation-only event
Exit code 0 is universal allow; block decision communicated through stdout JSON, not exit code
Hook failures surface as informational systemMessage, not blocking; graceful degradation principle
Hookify registers catch-all hooks per event type (no matcher) and filters tool_name in Python code
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var injected by Claude Code for portable plugin file references
Hook type in hooks.json is 'command' (external process); type field suggests other types may exist
tool_input stdin fields: Bash has command key; Edit has file_path/old_string/new_string; Write has file_path/content; MultiEdit has file_path/edits array
Stop event stdin includes transcript_path for full session inspection without inlining in JSON
UserPromptSubmit stdin includes user_prompt field for input-level guardrails
Multiple matching rules accumulate messages; blocking rules take full precedence over warnings
systemMessage surfaces text to model (not user terminal) for hook-injected context
Rule files use .claude/hookify.{name}.local.md pattern with hot-reload on next tool use
.local.md suffix convention indicates user-local gitignored config, separating from committed project config
Hook rule conditions use AND semantics; OR encoded via regex alternation in patterns
Regex matching is case-insensitive (re.IGNORECASE) and substring-based (re.search)
Compiled regex patterns cached with LRU size 128 per hook invocation
Simple 'pattern' frontmatter is sugar converted to single Condition with event-type-inferred field
Rules without conditions are non-matching (safety guard against catch-all misconfiguration)
Hook rule config uses custom YAML parser (stdlib only, no PyYAML dependency) for portability
tool_matcher supports exact match, pipe-separated OR, and wildcard '*' for per-tool filtering
stderr for diagnostics, stdout exclusively for JSON response; strict separation
hooks.json has top-level description field alongside hooks for plugin metadata
hook_event_name in stdin JSON used to select correct blocking response schema
No native SubagentStart or SessionStart hook events in hookify; four events are the public API
Rule files loaded from CWD .claude/ folder, making rule sets project-scoped
